title: Fix currency exchange rate fetching — app not working

requirements.text: Fix the exchange rate fetching in useQueries.ts so that live rates are successfully retrieved from open.er-api.com (with fallback to the Fawaz CDN API). Ensure network errors are handled gracefully and the UI displays a meaningful error state when all fetch attempts fail rather than silently breaking.
acceptanceCriteria: Exchange rates load successfully on app start without a blank or broken UI
If the primary API (open.er-api.com) fails, the fallback CDN API is attempted automatically
If all fetches fail, a user-facing error message is shown in the calculator UI
The converted amount, exchange rate, and last-updated timestamp all display correctly once rates are fetched

requirements.text: Verify and fix the backend Motoko actor's fetchExchangeRates HTTP outcall. Ensure the outbound GET request to the exchange rate API endpoint is correctly formed, the transform function properly sanitizes the HTTP response, and the actor returns valid rate data to the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: The Motoko actor's fetchExchangeRates function successfully completes an HTTP outcall to the configured API endpoint
The transform function correctly strips non-deterministic headers so the response passes consensus
The actor returns a valid, parseable exchange rate payload
No traps or unhandled errors occur during the HTTP outcall lifecycle

constraints: Do not change the overall UI layout or visual theme
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, or any component under frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Adding new currency pairs or expanding the supported currency list
Changing the financial dashboard visual theme
Adding new UI features beyond fixing the broken exchange rate flow
